title: The double half hitch (right to left)
subtitle: The right-to-left version for diagonal bars that climb from right to left
excerpt: The right-to-left double half hitch is two right-to-left half hitches in sequence on the same carrier cord. It is the mirror image of the left-to-right version. Combined with the left-to-right double half hitch, it builds the diagonal bars, V shapes, and chevrons that form the visual grammar of most macramé pattern work.
sourceNotes: The double half hitch is a standard macramé knot equivalent to the clove hitch in rope work. Observation-led.

content.text: is two right-to-left half hitches tied in sequence on the same carrier cord. Two half hitches lock together into a firm unit. This entry pairs with the published left-to-right double half hitch; together they produce

items.qty: 8 x 60 cm lengths for a practice grid
items.name: Cotton macramé cord, 3 mm single-strand

steps: Hold the carrier cord taut at a diagonal angled toward the lower left. Pin the top-right end to the board.
Take the rightmost working cord. Bring it over the carrier cord from right to left.
Loop it under the carrier cord, moving left to right, and bring it up through the loop it has formed. Pull to tighten. First half hitch seated.
With the same working cord, repeat: over the carrier right to left, under left to right, through the loop, tighten. Second half hitch seated.
One double half hitch is complete. Move to the next working cord to the left and repeat.
Continue leftward until all working cords are knotted onto the carrier.

content.text: To make a chevron row: mount eight cords (16 working ends) on a dowel. Take the centre-right cord as the right-to-left carrier. Knot the remaining cords to the right of centre onto it, working right to left. Take the centre-left cord as the left-to-right carrier. Knot the cords to the left of centre onto it, working left to right. The two carrier cords will meet at the centre bottom, where they are joined with a square knot or a final double half hitch.

attrs.intro: Common errors when building right-to-left rows.

items.fix: The carrier cord angle determines bar direction. For a right-to-left bar, hold the carrier at a diagonal toward the lower left before pinning.
items.cause: Carrier cord was angled toward the lower right instead of the lower left.
items.symptom: Bar slopes in the wrong direction

items.fix: Both half hitches must be in the same direction (both right to left). Check that the second pass also loops right over left before going through.
items.cause: Only one half hitch was completed instead of two, or the second was pulled in the wrong direction.
items.symptom: Knots feel loose and do not seat firmly

attrs.heading: Common problems
